title: SQS Dead Letter Queue Growth
spl:
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch" namespace="AWS/SQS" metric_name="ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible" QueueName="*dlq*"
| sort 0 _time
| streamstats window=12 global=f first(Average) as prev by QueueName
| eval growth=Average-prev
| where growth > 10
| table _time QueueName Average growth
description: DLQ depth growth means poison messages or downstream outages; rate-of-change highlights incidents faster than static thresholds.
value: DLQ depth growth means poison messages or downstream outages; rate-of-change highlights incidents faster than static thresholds.
implementation: Tag DLQs consistently for `*dlq*` matching or use explicit dimension. Alert on positive growth over 1h or depth exceeding SLO. Replay with caution after root-cause.
visualization: Line chart (DLQ depth), Single value (growth rate), Table (queue, depth).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
вАҐ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_aws`.
вАҐ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=aws:cloudwatch` (AWS/SQS вАФ ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible on DLQ ARNs).
вАҐ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 вАФ Configure data collection
Tag DLQs consistently for `*dlq*` matching or use explicit dimension. Alert on positive growth over 1h or depth exceeding SLO. Replay with caution after root-cause.

Step 2 вАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch" namespace="AWS/SQS" metric_name="ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible" QueueName="*dlq*"
| sort 0 _time
| streamstats window=12 global=f first(Average) as prev by QueueName
| eval growth=Average-prev
| where growth > 10
| table _time QueueName Average growth
```

Understanding this SPL

**SQS Dead Letter Queue Growth** вАФ DLQ depth growth means poison messages or downstream outages; rate-of-change highlights incidents faster than static thresholds.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=aws:cloudwatch` (AWS/SQS вАФ ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible on DLQ ARNs). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_aws`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedвАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: aws; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudwatch. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

вАҐ Scopes the data: index=aws, sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
вАҐ Orders rows with `sort` вАФ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
вАҐ `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by QueueName** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
вАҐ `eval` defines or adjusts **growth** вАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
вАҐ Filters the current rows with `where growth > 10` вАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
вАҐ Pipeline stage (see **SQS Dead Letter Queue Growth**): table _time QueueName Average growth

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 вАФ Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 вАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (DLQ depth), Single value (growth rate), Table (queue, depth).
grandmaExplanation: We keep an eye on sqs dead letter queue growth. The key performance numbers the cloud already exposes so the team can plan capacity and notice problems before user-visible outages. We want the right people to notice in time, not only when a customer or an auditor stumbles on it first.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` max(Performance.cpu_load_percent) as peak
  from datamodel=Performance.Performance
  by Performance.object Performance.host span=1h
| where isnotnull(peak)
| sort - peak
dataModelAcceleration: Performance: enable if `aws:cloudwatch` (or your metric store) is mapped to CIM Performance, or use `mstats` on the same CloudWatch metrics. Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM data models in `cimModels` once `Splunk_TA_aws` (and any CIM tags/aliases) map your fields; otherwise the `cimSpl` summary search may be empty. Step 2 on raw or metric indexes stays primary for most sites.
knownFalsePositives: Planned deploys, game-day tests, and vendor maintenance that you expect to move CPU, error, or lag metrics; compare with the change window.
